mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month trend, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy. This sustained tightening compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS*CPIAUCSL interaction and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over a 3-month rolling window signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk. This deterioration reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between PAYEMS and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) exhibit a positive trend when consumer confidence and disposable income remain robust. A month-over-month increase in RSAFS signals sustained aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS changes are followed by negative SPX returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, it signals supply-side weakness and reduced corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO declines and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and real GDP growth (GDPC1) jointly capture demand-side momentum. When both HOUST and GDPC1 show positive growth trends, the economy exhibits broad-based expansion that supports equity multiples and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average > 1250 thousand units AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 23400.294 billion chained dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between HOUST*GDPC1 interaction and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) serves as a leading indicator of labor market slack and wage pressure. When UNRATE rises above 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor demand and reduced consumer purchasing power, which compresses equity valuations through lower expected cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5%, or Rank IC between UNRATE and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) from elevated levels signals a shift toward monetary accommodation. This rate reduction lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and reduces refinancing costs for corporations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as markets reprice growth expectations.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or policy pivot
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS declines and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly capture supply-side capacity and labor utilization. When both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline simultaneously, it signals broad-based supply contraction and reduced productive capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets reprice earnings downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 2-month change < -1.0% AND PAYEMS 2-month change < -50 thousand persons
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO*PAYEMS interaction and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
